years.question: Your child has been born! They are crying with hunger. What will you give them to eat?
years.imagePrompt: A newborn baby, just born, peaceful and innocent

years.question: Your child is learning to walk. They keep falling down. How do you encourage them?
Your child wants a toy that another kid is playing with. What do you teach them?
It's time for preschool. Your child is nervous about meeting new kids. What do you say?
Your child asks you about their biggest fear. How do you help them face it?
Your child shows interest in a new hobby. What activity do you introduce them to?
First day of elementary school! Your child wants to bring something special. What do they bring?
Your child got into a disagreement with a friend. How do you guide them?
Your child wants to learn something new. What skill do they start developing?
Your child faces a challenging situation at school. How do they handle it?
Double digits! Your child wants to celebrate this milestone. How do they celebrate?
Your child is discovering their identity. What makes them feel most like themselves?
Middle school begins. Your child wants to join a group or club. Which one?
Your teen is going through changes. What positive habit do they develop?
Your teen faces peer pressure. How do they respond?
High school life is busy. What does your teen prioritize?
Your teen is thinking about their future. What path interests them most?
College applications and big decisions loom. What values guide your teen?
Graduation day! Your young adult reflects on their journey. What are they most proud of?
